summary.text: The dominant pattern today is sustained high attention on coding agents and MCP/tool-calling projects. DeepSeek-Reasonix (16.9k stars, +750 daily) and ECC (204k stars, +1.9k daily) continue to lead, both showing multi-day acceleration. Open Design (57.7k stars, +587 daily) also remains hot. MCP/tool-calling as a category averages 789 daily stars across 5 repos, while coding agents average 669 across 2. Meanwhile, local LLM inference (ncnn) and RAG (ml-engineering) show minimal daily growth, suggesting developer interest is shifting toward agentic tool-use layers rather than inference or retrieval infrastructure. The absence of new Product Hunt launches and only one cross-source signal (a Stanford study on AI outperforming law professors) indicates attention is consolidating around existing projects rather than new entrants.

projects.name: ncnn
projects.description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for the mobile platform

llmSummary.oneLiner: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with CPU and Vulkan GPU backends.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Deploying deep learning models efficiently on resource-constrained devices (phones, embedded systems, browsers) without heavy dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 2.3x acceleration ratio and a 7-star delta in the last 24 hours, despite a slight dip yesterday. With 23,318 stars and a recent release, it's gaining renewed attention as mobile and edge AI deployment becomes critical.
llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days suggest ongoing improvements. The README highlights new platform support (HarmonyOS, visionOS) and the pnnx tool for PyTorch/ONNX conversion, which likely drives interest.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is a mature, battle-tested framework from Tencent, used in major apps like WeChat and QQ. Its zero-dependency design and broad platform support make it a go-to for on-device AI, especially as edge inference gains momentum.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you need to run neural networks on mobile or embedded devices without heavy frameworks, ncnn offers a lightweight, high-performance solution with easy PyTorch/ONNX conversion via pnnx.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, ncnn signals a shift toward efficient on-device inference, reducing reliance on cloud APIs. It's particularly valuable for privacy-sensitive apps and real-time applications.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile developers, embedded systems engineers, AI researchers deploying models on edge devices, and anyone needing fast inference on Android, iOS, or Linux.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1,175 open issues, indicating active community support but also potential bugs or feature requests. Vulkan GPU support may not be available on all devices.

projects.name: ml-engineering

llmSummary.oneLiner: A comprehensive open book of methodologies, tools, and commands for training and fine-tuning large language and multi-modal models.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a centralized, practical reference for ML engineers tackling the complexities of large-scale model training, debugging, and inference, covering hardware, orchestration, and development workflows.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,015 stars and a sudden acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high), the repo is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour star delta of 20 and 7-day average of 6 indicate a sharp increase in attention, likely driven by the growing need for hands-on ML engineering guidance as LLM training becomes more accessible.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent push on 2026-05-18 and a talk on 2026-01-10 may have contributed to renewed interest.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a critical gap for ML engineers who need battle-tested, copy-paste-ready solutions for training large models, a topic that remains highly relevant as more teams attempt to train or fine-tune LLMs.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-bookmark resource for anyone involved in LLM/VLM training or inference, offering practical scripts and benchmarks that save hours of research.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward open, community-driven knowledge sharing in ML engineering, reducing the barrier for smaller teams to adopt large-scale training practices.
llmSummary.targetUsers: ML engineers, LLM/VLM training operators, and researchers working on large-scale model training and inference.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The content is based on the author's personal experience and may not cover all edge cases; users should validate scripts for their specific environments.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for prefix-cache stability and low token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a persistent, cost-efficient AI coding assistant that leverages DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce token spend during long sessions, while offering config-driven multi-model support, plugin extensibility, and sandboxed execution.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 16,880 stars, a 7-day delta of 7,990 stars, and a sustained 7-day average of 1,308.9 stars per day, this project is experiencing explosive organic growth. The 24-hour delta of 750 stars and acceleration ratio of 1 (high) indicate continued strong momentum without sudden spikes, suggesting a steady influx of users drawn to its DeepSeek-native design and Go rewrite.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project recently underwent a ground-up rewrite from TypeScript to Go (version 1.0), which is highlighted in the README as the new default branch. This rewrite likely improved performance, reduced binary size, and enhanced cache stability, driving renewed interest and adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines the popularity of DeepSeek models with a practical, config-driven terminal agent that promises lower costs via prefix caching. The Go rewrite delivers a single static binary, easy installation via npm/brew, and MCP plugin support, making it accessible and performant for developers seeking a local AI coding assistant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Reasonix offers a production-ready, DeepSeek-optimized coding agent with a plugin system and sandboxed execution. Developers can quickly set it up with `npm i -g reasonix` and configure it via TOML, benefiting from multi-model collaboration and low token costs.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward model-specific, cache-aware tooling that optimizes for cost and latency. The Go rewrite and MCP compatibility indicate a trend toward lightweight, cross-platform agents that integrate with existing developer workflows and plugin ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent with configurable tools, multi-model support, and sandboxed execution. Also relevant for teams seeking a cost-efficient, persistent assistant for long coding sessions.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 492 open issues, which may indicate growing pains or feature requests outpacing development. The legacy TypeScript branch is maintenance-only, so users on older versions need to migrate. Dependency on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior could be a risk if DeepSeek changes its pricing or caching model.

projects.name: ECC
projects.description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using AI coding agents face inconsistent behavior, high token costs, and lack of persistent memory across sessions. ECC provides a unified system of skills, hooks, memory optimization, and security scanning that works across different agent harnesses.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded from 182K to 203.5K stars in just days, with a 24h star delta of 2,588 and a 7-day average of 1,406.6. The acceleration ratio of 2.2 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by the v2.0.0-rc.1 release and growing adoption of multi-harness agent workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The v2.0.0-rc.1 release (Apr 2026) added a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, ECC 2.0 alpha (Rust control-plane), and expanded skill packs. This major update likely triggered the surge in attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As AI coding agents become mainstream, developers need a way to standardize and optimize their behavior. ECC's cross-harness compatibility and performance optimization features address a critical pain point, and the project's rapid iteration and community growth signal strong momentum.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: ECC is a must-evaluate tool for anyone using AI coding assistants in production. It offers immediate token savings, memory persistence, and security hardening, with a growing ecosystem of skills and integrations.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of ECC signals a shift from using AI agents in isolation to managing them as a fleet. Developers who adopt cross-harness optimization tools early will gain a competitive edge in productivity and code quality.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, or other AI coding agents who want to improve performance, reduce costs, and enforce security standards.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still in release candidate (v2.0.0-rc.1) and the ECC 2.0 alpha is experimental. Rapid growth may lead to instability or breaking changes. The single maintainer model is a bus-factor risk despite community contributions.

projects.name: open-design
projects.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Native desktop app. ⚡ 259+ Skills · ✨ 142+ Design Systems 🖼️ Web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Claude Code / OpenClaw / Codex / Cursor / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi & 17+ CLIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine, generating prototypes, decks, images, and videos from a single design system.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It replaces closed, pixel-pushing design tools with an agent-native workflow where coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) read a DESIGN.md brand contract and produce real CSS/HTML artifacts, enabling rapid, consistent design output without switching tools.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 57,739 stars, a 24-hour delta of +587, and a 7-day average of +815.6 stars per day, Open Design is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.1 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike, suggesting deep community adoption. The recent 0.9.0 release with an integrated Model Router lowers the barrier to entry, likely fueling continued interest.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The release of version 0.9.0 introduced an official Model Router built into the app, eliminating the need for extra configuration or API keys. This change, along with the launch of the Open Design Fellow program, has likely driven the recent surge in attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines the hottest trends—AI agents, local-first software, and open-source design tools—into a single product that works with 21+ coding agents. The promise of a Figma alternative for the agent era, plus the ability to export to HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4, appeals to developers and designers alike.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Open Design demonstrates a new paradigm: design as code, governed by a DESIGN.md contract, executed by any agent. Developers can integrate it into their existing agent workflows via MCP, making it a powerful addition to the AI-assisted development stack.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift from GUI-based design tools to agent-native, filesystem-driven design pipelines. For AI developers, it opens opportunities to build plugins, skills, and integrations that extend the design system ecosystem, and highlights the growing importance of MCP as a standard for tool interoperability.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers, designers, and product builders who want to generate design artifacts using AI agents, especially those already using Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or similar tools. Also teams seeking a local-first, open-source alternative to Figma or Claude Design.
llmSummary.riskNotes: As a rapidly growing open-source project, it may face scalability challenges in community management and plugin quality control. The reliance on multiple third-party agents could introduce compatibility issues. Additionally, the project's scope is broad, which may lead to feature creep or maintenance burden.

projects.name: nanobot
projects.description: Lightweight, open-source AI agent for your tools, chats, and workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An ultra-lightweight, open-source AI agent runtime that lets you own your entire agent stack with a small core, WebUI, chat channels, tools, memory, MCP, model routing, and deployment.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers and teams want to build and run custom AI agents without being locked into a single vendor or heavy framework. nanobot provides a minimal, extensible runtime that integrates multiple LLM providers, chat platforms, and tools, giving users full control over their agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 43,555 stars, a 7-day average delta of 58.1 stars/day, and a 24-hour delta of 59 stars, nanobot is experiencing sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio of 1.6 indicates strong, steady growth without a sudden spike. This consistent velocity suggests the project has crossed a threshold of mainstream developer interest and is being adopted as a go-to agent framework.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project released v0.2.1 on 2026-06-01, dubbed 'The Workbench Release,' which turned the WebUI into a daily agent workbench with clearer thought/response timelines, live file-edit activity, project workspaces, model and context controls, steadier sustained goals, CLI Apps + MCP extensions, and broader provider/channel support. This major feature release likely drove the recent star acceleration.
llmSummary.whyTrending: nanobot is trending because it offers a practical, lightweight alternative to heavy agent frameworks, with a focus on real-world usability: a built-in WebUI, multi-channel chat support (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WeChat, etc.), MCP integration, and a growing ecosystem of providers and tools. The rapid release cadence and active community engagement signal a project that is both innovative and responsive to user needs.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: nanobot is a strong candidate for developers who want to quickly prototype and deploy AI agents with minimal overhead. Its modular design and support for multiple LLM providers and chat platforms make it versatile for both personal projects and production deployments.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of nanobot signals a shift toward lightweight, self-hosted agent runtimes that prioritize developer control and ease of use over monolithic frameworks. Developers should consider adopting such tools to reduce vendor lock-in and accelerate agent development cycles.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams building AI agents for automation, customer support, personal assistants, or workflow integration who want a lightweight, open-source, and extensible runtime.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still relatively new (first release in early 2026) and may have stability or security issues in production. The high number of open issues (905) suggests active development but also potential growing pains. Dependency on multiple third-party APIs could introduce breaking changes.

projects.name: codeburn
projects.description: See where your AI coding tokens go. Interactive TUI dashboard for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor cost observability.

llmSummary.oneLiner: CodeBurn is a local TUI dashboard that tracks token usage and cost across 25+ AI coding tools, giving developers visibility into where their AI budget goes.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using multiple AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.) have no unified view of token consumption and spending. CodeBurn solves this by reading session data from disk, pricing calls via LiteLLM, and presenting an interactive terminal dashboard with breakdowns by tool, model, project, and provider.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 7,516 stars and a sustained 7-day average delta of 61.9 stars/day, CodeBurn is clearly resonating with the developer community. The 24h delta of 70 and acceleration of 1.4 indicate continued strong interest, not a flash in the pan. As AI coding tools proliferate, cost observability becomes a critical need.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release within 30 days and active commits suggest ongoing feature additions (like the new 'yield' command for tracking productive vs. reverted spend) that keep the project in the spotlight.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Developers are increasingly using multiple AI coding tools and facing unpredictable token costs. CodeBurn fills a clear gap by providing a local, privacy-respecting way to monitor and optimize spending, with support for 25+ providers and a polished TUI.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding tools and want to track costs without sending data to a third party, CodeBurn is a must-try. It's easy to install via npm or Homebrew, runs locally, and supports virtually every major AI coding assistant.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a maturing ecosystem where AI coding tools are becoming utilities that need cost management. Developers are no longer just experimenting — they're optimizing spend, and tools like CodeBurn will become standard in the AI-assisted developer workflow.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, GitHub Copilot, etc.) who want to monitor token usage, control costs, and compare model performance across tools.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on reading session data from disk, which may break if tool vendors change storage formats. Some providers (like Cursor) require estimation for hidden model usage, introducing potential inaccuracies. The project is relatively new and may have undiscovered bugs.

llmSummary.problemSolved: Eliminates the need to manually craft or collect prompt snippets for common development tasks by providing a searchable, installable catalog of structured SKILL.md playbooks that agents can execute directly.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 39,531 stars, a 7-day average delta of 119.3 stars/day, and a 24-hour delta of 105 stars, this project shows sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio of 1.1 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike. It's clearly resonating with the AI developer community as a go-to resource for agentic workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release V11.11.0 and continuous commits (last push June 2, 2026) suggest ongoing maintenance and feature additions that keep the community engaged.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses a universal pain point for AI coding assistant users: how to quickly equip agents with high-quality, domain-specific skills without reinventing the wheel. The breadth (1,494+ skills), ease of install (npx), and support for multiple tools make it a practical, time-saving resource.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a strong signal that the AI coding assistant ecosystem is maturing: developers want curated, installable skill libraries rather than ad-hoc prompts. The project's plugin system and tool-specific installers lower the barrier to adoption.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The success of this library suggests that the next wave of AI developer tooling will focus on composable, reusable skill packages. Developers should consider building or contributing to similar skill ecosystems to capture mindshare.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, etc.) who want to quickly add structured, reusable skills for tasks like web development, security, testing, DevOps, and product work.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project's long-term value depends on continued maintenance and community contributions. As AI assistants evolve, the skill format may need to adapt. There's also a risk of skill quality variance across the large catalog.

projects.name: rtk
projects.description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies

llmSummary.oneLiner: A Rust-based CLI proxy that intercepts command outputs and compresses them before they reach LLM context, cutting token usage by 60-90% on common dev commands.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM-powered coding tools (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor) waste tokens on verbose command output like git status, test logs, and directory listings. RTK filters, groups, truncates, and deduplicates these outputs in real-time, dramatically reducing token consumption and cost.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 58k+ stars and a 7-day average of 548 stars/day, RTK is one of the fastest-growing developer tools on GitHub. The 24h delta of 505 stars and acceleration of 1.2 indicate sustained, compounding interest. This is not a flash-in-the-pan — the project has maintained high velocity for weeks, suggesting deep adoption among AI-assisted developers.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent star velocity and recent release (within 30 days) suggest ongoing improvements and word-of-mouth spread through developer communities, Discord, and social media.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As LLM-powered coding tools become mainstream, token costs are a real pain point. RTK offers immediate, measurable savings (80%+ in typical sessions) with zero configuration overhead. It's a rare tool that pays for itself in minutes and works across all major AI coding agents.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, or any LLM-based coding assistant, installing RTK is a no-brainer. It integrates via a simple hook and transparently reduces your token spend by 60-90% without changing your workflow. The Rust binary is single-file, zero-dependency, and installs in seconds.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a new category of 'LLM middleware' — tools that sit between the developer and the AI to optimize cost and context. RTK's success suggests that as AI coding agents proliferate, the ecosystem around them (cost optimization, context management, output filtering) will become as important as the agents themselves.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, Codex) who want to reduce token costs and speed up LLM interactions. Especially valuable for teams with high-volume AI usage or those on pay-per-token plans.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1060 open issues, which is high relative to its size. Some users may encounter compatibility issues with specific commands or edge cases. The hook system may not work perfectly with all AI tools or shell environments. Also, the name collision with 'Rust Type Kit' on crates.io could cause confusion during installation.

projects.name: cc-switch
projects.description: A cross-platform desktop All-in-One assistant for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI & Hermes Agent. Only official website: ccswitch.io

llmSummary.oneLiner: A cross-platform desktop app that unifies management of multiple AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) with provider switching, skill management, and MCP support.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using multiple AI coding tools face fragmentation: each tool has its own CLI, API keys, configuration, and skill sets. CC Switch provides a single desktop interface to manage, switch, and orchestrate these tools, reducing context-switching overhead and simplifying provider management.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 89,652 stars and a sustained 7-day average of 1,188 stars/day, this project is experiencing massive organic growth. The 24-hour delta of 1,373 stars and acceleration of 1.2 (high but not sudden) indicate a steady, powerful momentum rather than a one-time spike. This level of sustained attention suggests deep community validation and real-world adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release within 30 days and active commits (pushed June 2, 2026) show ongoing development, but the star velocity has been consistently high for weeks, indicating cumulative word-of-mouth and viral spread among AI developers.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a real pain point for the growing number of developers using AI coding agents: tool fragmentation. By offering a unified UI with provider management, skill management, and MCP support, it becomes an essential utility. The sponsorship section with multiple API relay partners also creates an ecosystem effect, driving adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-watch project for anyone using multiple AI coding assistants. It reduces friction and could become the standard launcher for AI-assisted development workflows. The Tauri-based cross-platform design (Rust + TypeScript) also makes it performant and portable.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of multi-agent workflows demands unified tooling. CC Switch signals that the market is moving toward aggregation layers that manage multiple AI providers, skills, and MCP servers from one place. Developers should consider building integrations or plugins for this platform.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams who use Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw, or Hermes Agent and want a single desktop interface to manage them, switch providers, and share skills.
llmSummary.riskNotes: High star count but also 1,274 open issues — community expectations are high and may outpace development. The project relies on third-party API relay partners (sponsors), which could introduce vendor lock-in or reliability concerns. As a closed-source desktop app (despite being open-source), trust in the binary distribution is critical.

watchlistMovements.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
watchlistMovements.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for prefix-cache stability and low token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a persistent, cost-efficient AI coding assistant that leverages DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce token spend during long sessions, while offering config-driven multi-model support, plugin extensibility, and sandboxed execution.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 16,880 stars, a 7-day delta of 7,990 stars, and a sustained 7-day average of 1,308.9 stars per day, this project is experiencing explosive organic growth. The 24-hour delta of 750 stars and acceleration ratio of 1 (high) indicate continued strong momentum without sudden spikes, suggesting a steady influx of users drawn to its DeepSeek-native design and Go rewrite.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project recently underwent a ground-up rewrite from TypeScript to Go (version 1.0), which is highlighted in the README as the new default branch. This rewrite likely improved performance, reduced binary size, and enhanced cache stability, driving renewed interest and adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines the popularity of DeepSeek models with a practical, config-driven terminal agent that promises lower costs via prefix caching. The Go rewrite delivers a single static binary, easy installation via npm/brew, and MCP plugin support, making it accessible and performant for developers seeking a local AI coding assistant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Reasonix offers a production-ready, DeepSeek-optimized coding agent with a plugin system and sandboxed execution. Developers can quickly set it up with `npm i -g reasonix` and configure it via TOML, benefiting from multi-model collaboration and low token costs.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward model-specific, cache-aware tooling that optimizes for cost and latency. The Go rewrite and MCP compatibility indicate a trend toward lightweight, cross-platform agents that integrate with existing developer workflows and plugin ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent with configurable tools, multi-model support, and sandboxed execution. Also relevant for teams seeking a cost-efficient, persistent assistant for long coding sessions.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 492 open issues, which may indicate growing pains or feature requests outpacing development. The legacy TypeScript branch is maintenance-only, so users on older versions need to migrate. Dependency on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior could be a risk if DeepSeek changes its pricing or caching model.

watchlistMovements.name: ECC
watchlistMovements.description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using AI coding agents face inconsistent behavior, high token costs, and lack of persistent memory across sessions. ECC provides a unified system of skills, hooks, memory optimization, and security scanning that works across different agent harnesses.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded from 182K to 203.5K stars in just days, with a 24h star delta of 2,588 and a 7-day average of 1,406.6. The acceleration ratio of 2.2 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by the v2.0.0-rc.1 release and growing adoption of multi-harness agent workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The v2.0.0-rc.1 release (Apr 2026) added a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, ECC 2.0 alpha (Rust control-plane), and expanded skill packs. This major update likely triggered the surge in attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As AI coding agents become mainstream, developers need a way to standardize and optimize their behavior. ECC's cross-harness compatibility and performance optimization features address a critical pain point, and the project's rapid iteration and community growth signal strong momentum.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: ECC is a must-evaluate tool for anyone using AI coding assistants in production. It offers immediate token savings, memory persistence, and security hardening, with a growing ecosystem of skills and integrations.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of ECC signals a shift from using AI agents in isolation to managing them as a fleet. Developers who adopt cross-harness optimization tools early will gain a competitive edge in productivity and code quality.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, or other AI coding agents who want to improve performance, reduce costs, and enforce security standards.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still in release candidate (v2.0.0-rc.1) and the ECC 2.0 alpha is experimental. Rapid growth may lead to instability or breaking changes. The single maintainer model is a bus-factor risk despite community contributions.

watchlistMovements.name: codeburn
watchlistMovements.description: See where your AI coding tokens go. Interactive TUI dashboard for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor cost observability.

llmSummary.oneLiner: CodeBurn is a local TUI dashboard that tracks token usage and cost across 25+ AI coding tools, giving developers visibility into where their AI budget goes.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using multiple AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.) have no unified view of token consumption and spending. CodeBurn solves this by reading session data from disk, pricing calls via LiteLLM, and presenting an interactive terminal dashboard with breakdowns by tool, model, project, and provider.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 7,516 stars and a sustained 7-day average delta of 61.9 stars/day, CodeBurn is clearly resonating with the developer community. The 24h delta of 70 and acceleration of 1.4 indicate continued strong interest, not a flash in the pan. As AI coding tools proliferate, cost observability becomes a critical need.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release within 30 days and active commits suggest ongoing feature additions (like the new 'yield' command for tracking productive vs. reverted spend) that keep the project in the spotlight.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Developers are increasingly using multiple AI coding tools and facing unpredictable token costs. CodeBurn fills a clear gap by providing a local, privacy-respecting way to monitor and optimize spending, with support for 25+ providers and a polished TUI.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding tools and want to track costs without sending data to a third party, CodeBurn is a must-try. It's easy to install via npm or Homebrew, runs locally, and supports virtually every major AI coding assistant.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a maturing ecosystem where AI coding tools are becoming utilities that need cost management. Developers are no longer just experimenting — they're optimizing spend, and tools like CodeBurn will become standard in the AI-assisted developer workflow.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, GitHub Copilot, etc.) who want to monitor token usage, control costs, and compare model performance across tools.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on reading session data from disk, which may break if tool vendors change storage formats. Some providers (like Cursor) require estimation for hidden model usage, introducing potential inaccuracies. The project is relatively new and may have undiscovered bugs.

watchlistMovements.name: rtk
watchlistMovements.description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies

llmSummary.oneLiner: A Rust-based CLI proxy that intercepts command outputs and compresses them before they reach LLM context, cutting token usage by 60-90% on common dev commands.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM-powered coding tools (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor) waste tokens on verbose command output like git status, test logs, and directory listings. RTK filters, groups, truncates, and deduplicates these outputs in real-time, dramatically reducing token consumption and cost.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 58k+ stars and a 7-day average of 548 stars/day, RTK is one of the fastest-growing developer tools on GitHub. The 24h delta of 505 stars and acceleration of 1.2 indicate sustained, compounding interest. This is not a flash-in-the-pan — the project has maintained high velocity for weeks, suggesting deep adoption among AI-assisted developers.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent star velocity and recent release (within 30 days) suggest ongoing improvements and word-of-mouth spread through developer communities, Discord, and social media.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As LLM-powered coding tools become mainstream, token costs are a real pain point. RTK offers immediate, measurable savings (80%+ in typical sessions) with zero configuration overhead. It's a rare tool that pays for itself in minutes and works across all major AI coding agents.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, or any LLM-based coding assistant, installing RTK is a no-brainer. It integrates via a simple hook and transparently reduces your token spend by 60-90% without changing your workflow. The Rust binary is single-file, zero-dependency, and installs in seconds.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a new category of 'LLM middleware' — tools that sit between the developer and the AI to optimize cost and context. RTK's success suggests that as AI coding agents proliferate, the ecosystem around them (cost optimization, context management, output filtering) will become as important as the agents themselves.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, Codex) who want to reduce token costs and speed up LLM interactions. Especially valuable for teams with high-volume AI usage or those on pay-per-token plans.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1060 open issues, which is high relative to its size. Some users may encounter compatibility issues with specific commands or edge cases. The hook system may not work perfectly with all AI tools or shell environments. Also, the name collision with 'Rust Type Kit' on crates.io could cause confusion during installation.

modelDemoSignals.title: Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
modelDemoSignals.description: Vision language models (VLMs) excel at many tasks but still struggle with spatial reasoning when critical information is not directly observable. Many such problems require imaginative perception: inferring what would be seen from an unseen viewpoint, tracing paths through occluded spaces, or integrating partial observations into a coherent spatial representation. We introduce Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), intermediate perceptual representations that externalize what a VLM would perceive
modelDemoSignals.summary: Vision language models (VLMs) excel at many tasks but still struggle with spatial reasoning when critical information is not directly observable. Many such problems require imaginative perception: inferring what would be seen from an unseen viewpoint, tracing paths through occluded spaces, or integrating partial observations into a coherent spatial representation. We introduce Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), intermediate perceptual representations that externalize what a VLM would perceive under alternative spatial configurations while remaining consistent with the observed input. To study this capability, we formulate three tasks, Perspective Taking (PET), Path Tracing (PT), and Multiview Counting (MVC), and construct datasets of approximately 20K examples with ground truth imaginations, answers, and evaluation benchmarks. Using the unified VLM BAGEL as the backbone, IPT supervision consistently improves spatial reasoning and often outperforms textual chain of thought training, even without generating images at inference time. On MVC, IPT improves accuracy by 3.4% and achieves competitive performance with strong closed-source models on PT. We further find that combining IPT and label-only supervision yields additional gains, whereas textual chain of thought can substantially degrade performance, suggesting a modality mismatch when spatial computation is forced through language. Overall, IPT provides a principled supervision signal for reasoning about unobserved spatial structure, improving generalization while producing interpretable intermediate representations.
modelDemoSignals.author: Mahtab Bigverdi, Lindsey Li, Weikai Huang, Yiming Liu, Jaemin Cho, Jieyu Zhang, Tuhin Kundu, Chris Dangjoo Kim, Zelun Luo, Linda Shapiro, Ranjay Krishna

modelDemoSignals.title: Skill-RM: Unifying Heterogeneous Evaluation Criteria via Agent Skill
modelDemoSignals.description: Reward models (RMs) provide critical feedback signals for LLM post-training, notably in reinforced fine-tuning (RFT) and reinforcement learning (RL) pipelines. However, current reward evaluation relies on heterogeneous criteria such as rule-based verifiers, ground-truth references, procedural checklists, and complex rubrics, where a unified mechanism to integrate all types of evidence remains unexplored. To this end, we propose Skill Reward Model (Skill-RM), a unified framework that reformulates
modelDemoSignals.summary: Reward models (RMs) provide critical feedback signals for LLM post-training, notably in reinforced fine-tuning (RFT) and reinforcement learning (RL) pipelines. However, current reward evaluation relies on heterogeneous criteria such as rule-based verifiers, ground-truth references, procedural checklists, and complex rubrics, where a unified mechanism to integrate all types of evidence remains unexplored. To this end, we propose Skill Reward Model (Skill-RM), a unified framework that reformulates reward modeling as the execution of a reusable Reward-Evaluation Skill. By treating reward computation as a structured agentic task, Skill-RM provides a consistent interface to orchestrate heterogeneous resources, dynamically selecting and aggregating evidence tailored to the specific requirements of each input. This approach enables the reward model to move beyond static evaluation, ensuring consistency and transparency across diverse tasks. Extensive experiments on reward benchmarks and downstream applications, including best-of-N selection and reinforcement learning, demonstrate that Skill-RM consistently outperforms traditional judge baselines. Our findings suggest that Skill-RM not only provides a unified solution for reward modeling but also achieves superior performance through the strategic and dynamic orchestration of evidence. The code is at https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/Skill-RM.
modelDemoSignals.author: Tao Chen, Gangwei Jiang, Pengyu Cheng, Siyuan Huang, Yihao Liu, Jingwei Ni, Jiaqi Guo, Mengyu Zhou, Kai Tang, Junling Liu, Qinliang Su, Xiaoxi Jiang, Guanjun Jiang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, fine-tuning, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
modelDemoSignals.description: The past few decades have witnessed significant advances in the design of machine learning algorithms, from early studies on task-specific shallow models to more general deep Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite showing promising results in tasks that require instant prediction or in-context learning, existing models lack the ability to continually learn and effectively transfer their temporal in-context knowledge to their long-term parameters. Inspired by human learning process, we introduce a
modelDemoSignals.summary: The past few decades have witnessed significant advances in the design of machine learning algorithms, from early studies on task-specific shallow models to more general deep Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite showing promising results in tasks that require instant prediction or in-context learning, existing models lack the ability to continually learn and effectively transfer their temporal in-context knowledge to their long-term parameters. Inspired by human learning process, we introduce a ''Sleep'' paradigm that allows the models to continually learn, distill their short-term fragile memories into stable long-term knowledge with replay, and recursively improve themselves with ''Dreaming'' process. In more detail, sleep consists of two stages: (1) Memory Consolidation: an upward distillation process, called Knowledge Seeding, where the memories of a smaller-self are distilled into a larger network to provide more capacity while preserving the knowledge. As a proof of concept, we present a new Generalized Distillation process for {Knowledge Seeding} (i.e., the combination of on-policy distillation with Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based imitation learning); (2) Dreaming: a self-improvement phase, where the model uses RL to generate a curriculum of synthetic data to rehearse new knowledge and refine existing capabilities without human supervision. Our experiments on long-horizon, continual learning, knowledge incorporation, and few-shot generalization tasks support the importance of the sleep stage.
modelDemoSignals.author: Ali Behrouz, Farnoosh Hashemi, Vahab Mirrokni
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag

modelDemoSignals.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
modelDemoSignals.description: Reliable uncertainty communication is critical to the trustworthiness of LLMs, yet faithful calibration (FC)--the alignment between models' intrinsic and (linguistically) expressed confidence--is a persistent failure mode. modelDemoSignals.summary: Reliable uncertainty communication is critical to the trustworthiness of LLMs, yet faithful calibration (FC)--the alignment between models' intrinsic and (linguistically) expressed confidence--is a persistent failure mode. This challenge is key for large reasoning models (LRMs), whose extended reasoning traces are often interpreted by users as evidence of deliberation, competence, and confidence. Despite the importance of FC and wide usage of LRMs, the extent to which LRMs can faithfully express their confidence remains poorly understood. Moreover, the prevailing paradigm to measure FC does not generalize well to the long chain-of-thought outputs generated by LRMs, which tend to lack clear step boundaries, involve inconsistent step structure, and encode complex conditional dependencies throughout the trace--complicating estimation of intrinsic confidence. To address this challenge, we introduce a novel framework to systematically quantify FC of LRMs. Our framework analyzes linguistic decisiveness relative to three sources of internal uncertainty, based on token probabilities, hidden states, and sampled response consistency. We also devise a prefix-conditioned sampling approach to control for conditional and structural variation across traces. Applying our framework to a diverse suite of leading models, datasets, and prompts, we find that faithful confidence expression is a significant challenge for LRMs. Reasoning behaviors do not automatically translate to improved FC, and prompt interventions for non-reasoning models do not improve faithfulness in the reasoning setting. Different confidence estimators further produce divergent assessments of the same traces, revealing fragility in prior evaluation methodologies. Taken together, our work establishes FC as a distinct reliability and alignment target for LRMs, particularly as such systems are increasingly deployed in high-stakes contexts.
modelDemoSignals.author: Areeb Gani, Asal Meskin, Gabrielle Kaili-May Liu, Arman Cohan
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, rag, alignment, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
modelDemoSignals.description: Rubric-based RL is a promising route for extending reinforcement learning beyond verifiable rewards, yet existing methods optimize rubrics while treating the query distribution as fixed. We identify a structural bottleneck: rubric quality is constrained by query structure. Open-ended queries yield vague rubrics; naively narrowing them introduces fabricated references that no model can verify, so all responses fail and training receives no reward signal. We present QUBRIC, a framework that co-des
modelDemoSignals.summary: Rubric-based RL is a promising route for extending reinforcement learning beyond verifiable rewards, yet existing methods optimize rubrics while treating the query distribution as fixed. We identify a structural bottleneck: rubric quality is constrained by query structure. Open-ended queries yield vague rubrics; naively narrowing them introduces fabricated references that no model can verify, so all responses fail and training receives no reward signal. We present QUBRIC, a framework that co-designs queries and rubrics. Teacher-derived key points ground the rewriting of open-ended queries into scenario-based, evaluable questions. Contrastive rubric generation then turns teacher-policy gaps into query-level criteria, and learnability filtering retains only informative query-rubric pairs for GRPO training. QUBRIC achieves a +5.5 point gain on ArenaHard over the SFT baseline. Trained only on instruction-following data, it further transfers to three held-out benchmarks spanning legal, moral, and narrative reasoning (+6.3 points on average), with improvements concentrated in reasoning-related dimensions. These results provide evidence that co-designing queries and rubrics can make rubric-based RL a practical complement to RLVR beyond strictly verifiable tasks.
modelDemoSignals.author: Rongzhi Zhang, Rui Feng, Zhihan Zhang, Jingfeng Yang, Qingyu Yin, Xin Liu, Zixuan Zhang, Priyanka Nigam, Bing Yin, Tuo Zhao, Chao Zhang

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能
aihotHighlights.summary: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能，允许模型在运行时即兴创建和协调多智能体框架来处理复杂任务。该功能通过执行特定的 JavaScript 文件来生成和协调拥有独立上下文窗口的子代理，可解决单一上下文窗口中长时间执行任务可能出现的智能惰性等问题。工作流适用于研究、安全分析、代码审查等场景，通常消耗更多 token，更适合高价值复杂任务，其最佳实践仍在发展中。

items.title: 消息称 DeepSeek 首轮融资拟筹集 500 亿元，腾讯、宁德时代等参投
items.description: DeepSeek 计划在首轮融资中从腾讯、宁德时代等投资方处筹集约 500 亿元人民币。融资落地后，其投后估值预计在 3500 亿至 4000 亿元。创始人梁文峰将出资 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代分别拟投资 100 亿元和 50 亿元，成为最大外部投资者。DeepSeek 去年凭借 V3 大模型与 R1 推理模型获得广泛认可。此次融资凸显了中国正打造从大模型到算力能源基建的全链条 AI 产业。

items.title: Sensor Tower：OpenAI 旗下 ChatGPT 月活已破 10 亿，史上最快
items.description: 据市场情报机构 Sensor Tower 估计，OpenAI 旗下 ChatGPT 全球月活跃用户在 2025 年 5 月突破 10 亿，成为达成此里程碑最快的应用，增速超过 Google Maps、TikTok 等。同期，其竞争对手 Anthropic 的 Claude 月活达 5600 万，同比增幅约 640%，增长势头迅猛。数据显示，部分用户已开始在 Claude 与 ChatGPT 间切换使用。在达成用户里程碑之际，Anthropic 已秘密递交 IPO 申请，OpenAI 也计划筹备上市。

items.title: 智能体工程实战窍门全录
items.description: 该内容源自@mvanhorn的分享，介绍了"智能体工程"如何重塑软件开发。其核心是从"人主导编码"转向"人主导方向、智能体执行"，中心从IDE变为终端与计划文件。方法论遵循Research → Plan → Work循环，核心是让plan.md约束智能体行为。分享者总结了22条实战技巧，涵盖规划、并行执行、输入方式、远程控制等方面，并列出了完整的工具栈。

items.title: NVIDIA 推出 NemoClaw 平台，助力工业软件厂商构建自主 AI 工程师
items.description: 在 COMPUTEX 上，NVIDIA 发布了 NemoClaw 平台，这是一个用于构建专业、长时间运行 AI 智能体的开放蓝图。该平台提供安全运行时、前沿模型支持以及多种编排框架集成选项，可通过 DGX Spark、数据中心或云端部署。其核心开源运行时 OpenShell 实施基于策略的安全管控，规范智能体对文件、网络和工具的访问。Cadence、达索系统、西门子、Synopsys 等十多家工业软件厂商正基于 NemoClaw 构建用于 CAE 和 EDA 工作流的自主 AI 工程师，旨在将原本需数周的仿真与设计任务压缩至数小时。

items.title: 消息称 DeepSeek 首轮融资拟筹集 500 亿元，腾讯、宁德时代等参投
items.description: DeepSeek 计划在首轮融资中从腾讯、宁德时代等投资方处筹集约 500 亿元人民币。融资落地后，其投后估值预计在 3500 亿至 4000 亿元。创始人梁文峰将出资 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代分别拟投资 100 亿元和 50 亿元，成为最大外部投资者。DeepSeek 去年凭借 V3 大模型与 R1 推理模型获得广泛认可。此次融资凸显了中国正打造从大模型到算力能源基建的全链条 AI 产业。

trendEntities.title: 消息称 DeepSeek 首轮融资拟筹集 500 亿元，腾讯、宁德时代等参投
normalizedKeys: title:消息称 deepseek 首轮融资拟筹集 500 亿元 腾讯 宁德时代等参投

items.title: 消息称 DeepSeek 首轮融资拟筹集 500 亿元，腾讯、宁德时代等参投
items.summary: DeepSeek 计划在首轮融资中从腾讯、宁德时代等投资方处筹集约 500 亿元人民币。融资落地后，其投后估值预计在 3500 亿至 4000 亿元。创始人梁文峰将出资 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代分别拟投资 100 亿元和 50 亿元，成为最大外部投资者。DeepSeek 去年凭借 V3 大模型与 R1 推理模型获得广泛认可。此次融资凸显了中国正打造从大模型到算力能源基建的全链条 AI 产业。

trendEntities.title: Sensor Tower：OpenAI 旗下 ChatGPT 月活已破 10 亿，史上最快
normalizedKeys: title:sensor tower openai 旗下 chatgpt 月活已破 10 亿 史上最快

items.title: Sensor Tower：OpenAI 旗下 ChatGPT 月活已破 10 亿，史上最快
items.summary: 据市场情报机构 Sensor Tower 估计，OpenAI 旗下 ChatGPT 全球月活跃用户在 2025 年 5 月突破 10 亿，成为达成此里程碑最快的应用，增速超过 Google Maps、TikTok 等。同期，其竞争对手 Anthropic 的 Claude 月活达 5600 万，同比增幅约 640%，增长势头迅猛。数据显示，部分用户已开始在 Claude 与 ChatGPT 间切换使用。在达成用户里程碑之际，Anthropic 已秘密递交 IPO 申请，OpenAI 也计划筹备上市。

trendEntities.title: NVIDIA 推出 NemoClaw 平台，助力工业软件厂商构建自主 AI 工程师
normalizedKeys: title:nvidia 推出 nemoclaw 平台 助力工业软件厂商构建自主 ai 工程师

items.title: NVIDIA 推出 NemoClaw 平台，助力工业软件厂商构建自主 AI 工程师
items.summary: 在 COMPUTEX 上，NVIDIA 发布了 NemoClaw 平台，这是一个用于构建专业、长时间运行 AI 智能体的开放蓝图。该平台提供安全运行时、前沿模型支持以及多种编排框架集成选项，可通过 DGX Spark、数据中心或云端部署。其核心开源运行时 OpenShell 实施基于策略的安全管控，规范智能体对文件、网络和工具的访问。Cadence、达索系统、西门子、Synopsys 等十多家工业软件厂商正基于 NemoClaw 构建用于 CAE 和 EDA 工作流的自主 AI 工程师，旨在将原本需数周的仿真与设计任务压缩至数小时。

trendEntities.title: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能
normalizedKeys: title:claude code 新增动态工作流功能

items.title: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能
items.summary: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能，允许模型在运行时即兴创建和协调多智能体框架来处理复杂任务。该功能通过执行特定的 JavaScript 文件来生成和协调拥有独立上下文窗口的子代理，可解决单一上下文窗口中长时间执行任务可能出现的智能惰性等问题。工作流适用于研究、安全分析、代码审查等场景，通常消耗更多 token，更适合高价值复杂任务，其最佳实践仍在发展中。

sourceItems.title: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能
sourceItems.summary: Claude Code 新增动态工作流功能，允许模型在运行时即兴创建和协调多智能体框架来处理复杂任务。该功能通过执行特定的 JavaScript 文件来生成和协调拥有独立上下文窗口的子代理，可解决单一上下文窗口中长时间执行任务可能出现的智能惰性等问题。工作流适用于研究、安全分析、代码审查等场景，通常消耗更多 token，更适合高价值复杂任务，其最佳实践仍在发展中。

items.summary: Alphabet宣布拟通过股权融资800亿美元，用于扩展AI基础设施。Anthropic已秘密提交IPO申请，在上市竞赛中领先于竞争对手OpenAI。此外，SpaceX正与华尔街机构协商其IPO的承销费用，HPE则因AI基础设施需求旺盛，年度销售预期超出市场估计。

items.title: Claude Platform 新增 CLI 工具
items.summary: 我们为 Claude Platform 添加了一个 CLI，使每个 API 端点都可以从你的终端运行。 调用 Messages API，启动 Claude 托管智能体，并将结果直接管道传输到你的 shell。 ant CLI 被使用 claude-api 技能的编码智能体（Claude Code）很好地理解。

trendEntities.title: 微软首款高级推理AI模型MAI-Thinking-1发布
normalizedKeys: title:微软首款高级推理ai模型mai thinking 1发布

items.title: 微软首款高级推理AI模型MAI-Thinking-1发布
items.summary: 微软在Build 2026上发布了其首款高级推理AI模型MAI-Thinking-1。该模型被定位为"中等规模"，能在"关键"软件工程基准测试中达到领先模型的水平。微软称其完全从头使用干净数据进行训练，未涉及从第三方模型进行知识蒸馏。这标志着微软在自研AI模型上迈出重要一步，此前其主要依赖OpenAI。近期两家公司已重新协商合作协议，关系有所松绑。

items.title: GitHub Copilot应用：智能体原生的桌面体验
items.summary: 在微软 Build 2026 大会上，GitHub 发布了新的工具和更新，并将 Copilot 应用定位为"智能体原生的桌面体验"。其核心目标是让 AI 智能体能够以用户已经习惯的方式进行工作。

trendEntities.title: Claude Code团队实践：智能体编程如何重塑工程组织与流程
normalizedKeys: title:claude code团队实践 智能体编程如何重塑工程组织与流程

items.title: Claude Code团队实践：智能体编程如何重塑工程组织与流程
items.summary: 在Code w/ Claude SF 2026活动上，Claude Code工程团队分享了将智能体编程设为默认工作方式后带来的流程与结构变革。核心变化包括：规划转向即时（JIT）模式，强调快速原型与反馈；上下文收集变为"先问Claude"；代码审查中Claude处理风格与测试，人工专注于法律、安全等专业判断。新范式下，工程瓶颈从编写代码转向验证、审查与安全维护。

Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
normalizedKeys: title:imaginative perception tokens enhance spatial reasoning in multimodal language models

items.title: Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
items.description: Vision language models (VLMs) excel at many tasks but still struggle with spatial reasoning when critical information is not directly observable. Many such problems require imaginative perception: inferring what would be seen from an unseen viewpoint, tracing paths through occluded spaces, or integrating partial observations into a coherent spatial representation. We introduce Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), intermediate perceptual representations that externalize what a VLM would perceive
items.summary: Vision language models (VLMs) excel at many tasks but still struggle with spatial reasoning when critical information is not directly observable. Many such problems require imaginative perception: inferring what would be seen from an unseen viewpoint, tracing paths through occluded spaces, or integrating partial observations into a coherent spatial representation. We introduce Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), intermediate perceptual representations that externalize what a VLM would perceive under alternative spatial configurations while remaining consistent with the observed input. To study this capability, we formulate three tasks, Perspective Taking (PET), Path Tracing (PT), and Multiview Counting (MVC), and construct datasets of approximately 20K examples with ground truth imaginations, answers, and evaluation benchmarks. Using the unified VLM BAGEL as the backbone, IPT supervision consistently improves spatial reasoning and often outperforms textual chain of thought training, even without generating images at inference time. On MVC, IPT improves accuracy by 3.4% and achieves competitive performance with strong closed-source models on PT. We further find that combining IPT and label-only supervision yields additional gains, whereas textual chain of thought can substantially degrade performance, suggesting a modality mismatch when spatial computation is forced through language. Overall, IPT provides a principled supervision signal for reasoning about unobserved spatial structure, improving generalization while producing interpretable intermediate representations.
items.author: Mahtab Bigverdi, Lindsey Li, Weikai Huang, Yiming Liu, Jaemin Cho, Jieyu Zhang, Tuhin Kundu, Chris Dangjoo Kim, Zelun Luo, Linda Shapiro, Ranjay Krishna
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark, evaluation

normalizedKeys: title:skill rm unifying heterogeneous evaluation criteria via agent skill

items.title: Skill-RM: Unifying Heterogeneous Evaluation Criteria via Agent Skill
items.description: Reward models (RMs) provide critical feedback signals for LLM post-training, notably in reinforced fine-tuning (RFT) and reinforcement learning (RL) pipelines. However, current reward evaluation relies on heterogeneous criteria such as rule-based verifiers, ground-truth references, procedural checklists, and complex rubrics, where a unified mechanism to integrate all types of evidence remains unexplored. To this end, we propose Skill Reward Model (Skill-RM), a unified framework that reformulates
items.summary: Reward models (RMs) provide critical feedback signals for LLM post-training, notably in reinforced fine-tuning (RFT) and reinforcement learning (RL) pipelines. However, current reward evaluation relies on heterogeneous criteria such as rule-based verifiers, ground-truth references, procedural checklists, and complex rubrics, where a unified mechanism to integrate all types of evidence remains unexplored. To this end, we propose Skill Reward Model (Skill-RM), a unified framework that reformulates reward modeling as the execution of a reusable Reward-Evaluation Skill. By treating reward computation as a structured agentic task, Skill-RM provides a consistent interface to orchestrate heterogeneous resources, dynamically selecting and aggregating evidence tailored to the specific requirements of each input. This approach enables the reward model to move beyond static evaluation, ensuring consistency and transparency across diverse tasks. Extensive experiments on reward benchmarks and downstream applications, including best-of-N selection and reinforcement learning, demonstrate that Skill-RM consistently outperforms traditional judge baselines. Our findings suggest that Skill-RM not only provides a unified solution for reward modeling but also achieves superior performance through the strategic and dynamic orchestration of evidence. The code is at https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/Skill-RM.
items.author: Tao Chen, Gangwei Jiang, Pengyu Cheng, Siyuan Huang, Yihao Liu, Jingwei Ni, Jiaqi Guo, Mengyu Zhou, Kai Tang, Junling Liu, Qinliang Su, Xiaoxi Jiang, Guanjun Jiang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, fine-tuning, benchmark, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
normalizedKeys: title:language models need sleep learning to self modify and consolidate memories

items.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
items.description: The past few decades have witnessed significant advances in the design of machine learning algorithms, from early studies on task-specific shallow models to more general deep Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite showing promising results in tasks that require instant prediction or in-context learning, existing models lack the ability to continually learn and effectively transfer their temporal in-context knowledge to their long-term parameters. Inspired by human learning process, we introduce a
items.summary: The past few decades have witnessed significant advances in the design of machine learning algorithms, from early studies on task-specific shallow models to more general deep Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite showing promising results in tasks that require instant prediction or in-context learning, existing models lack the ability to continually learn and effectively transfer their temporal in-context knowledge to their long-term parameters. Inspired by human learning process, we introduce a ''Sleep'' paradigm that allows the models to continually learn, distill their short-term fragile memories into stable long-term knowledge with replay, and recursively improve themselves with ''Dreaming'' process. In more detail, sleep consists of two stages: (1) Memory Consolidation: an upward distillation process, called Knowledge Seeding, where the memories of a smaller-self are distilled into a larger network to provide more capacity while preserving the knowledge. As a proof of concept, we present a new Generalized Distillation process for {Knowledge Seeding} (i.e., the combination of on-policy distillation with Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based imitation learning); (2) Dreaming: a self-improvement phase, where the model uses RL to generate a curriculum of synthetic data to rehearse new knowledge and refine existing capabilities without human supervision. Our experiments on long-horizon, continual learning, knowledge incorporation, and few-shot generalization tasks support the importance of the sleep stage.
items.author: Ali Behrouz, Farnoosh Hashemi, Vahab Mirrokni
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag

trendEntities.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
normalizedKeys: title:quantifying faithful confidence expression in large reasoning models

items.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
items.description: Reliable uncertainty communication is critical to the trustworthiness of LLMs, yet faithful calibration (FC)--the alignment between models' intrinsic and (linguistically) expressed confidence--is a persistent failure mode. This challenge is key for large reasoning models (LRMs), whose extended reasoning traces are often interpreted by users as evidence of deliberation, competence, and confidence. Despite the importance of FC and wide usage of LRMs, the extent to which LRMs can faithfully express
items.summary: Reliable uncertainty communication is critical to the trustworthiness of LLMs, yet faithful calibration (FC)--the alignment between models' intrinsic and (linguistically) expressed confidence--is a persistent failure mode. This challenge is key for large reasoning models (LRMs), whose extended reasoning traces are often interpreted by users as evidence of deliberation, competence, and confidence. Despite the importance of FC and wide usage of LRMs, the extent to which LRMs can faithfully express their confidence remains poorly understood. Moreover, the prevailing paradigm to measure FC does not generalize well to the long chain-of-thought outputs generated by LRMs, which tend to lack clear step boundaries, involve inconsistent step structure, and encode complex conditional dependencies throughout the trace--complicating estimation of intrinsic confidence. To address this challenge, we introduce a novel framework to systematically quantify FC of LRMs. Our framework analyzes linguistic decisiveness relative to three sources of internal uncertainty, based on token probabilities, hidden states, and sampled response consistency. We also devise a prefix-conditioned sampling approach to control for conditional and structural variation across traces. Applying our framework to a diverse suite of leading models, datasets, and prompts, we find that faithful confidence expression is a significant challenge for LRMs. Reasoning behaviors do not automatically translate to improved FC, and prompt interventions for non-reasoning models do not improve faithfulness in the reasoning setting. Different confidence estimators further produce divergent assessments of the same traces, revealing fragility in prior evaluation methodologies. Taken together, our work establishes FC as a distinct reliability and alignment target for LRMs, particularly as such systems are increasingly deployed in high-stakes contexts.
items.author: Areeb Gani, Asal Meskin, Gabrielle Kaili-May Liu, Arman Cohan
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, rag, alignment, evaluation

trendEntities.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
normalizedKeys: title:qubric co designing queries and rubrics for rl beyond verifiable rewards

items.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
items.description: Rubric-based RL is a promising route for extending reinforcement learning beyond verifiable rewards, yet existing methods optimize rubrics while treating the query distribution as fixed. We identify a structural bottleneck: rubric quality is constrained by query structure. Open-ended queries yield vague rubrics; naively narrowing them introduces fabricated references that no model can verify, so all responses fail and training receives no reward signal. We present QUBRIC, a framework that co-des
items.summary: Rubric-based RL is a promising route for extending reinforcement learning beyond verifiable rewards, yet existing methods optimize rubrics while treating the query distribution as fixed. We identify a structural bottleneck: rubric quality is constrained by query structure. Open-ended queries yield vague rubrics; naively narrowing them introduces fabricated references that no model can verify, so all responses fail and training receives no reward signal. We present QUBRIC, a framework that co-designs queries and rubrics. Teacher-derived key points ground the rewriting of open-ended queries into scenario-based, evaluable questions. Contrastive rubric generation then turns teacher-policy gaps into query-level criteria, and learnability filtering retains only informative query-rubric pairs for GRPO training. QUBRIC achieves a +5.5 point gain on ArenaHard over the SFT baseline. Trained only on instruction-following data, it further transfers to three held-out benchmarks spanning legal, moral, and narrative reasoning (+6.3 points on average), with improvements concentrated in reasoning-related dimensions. These results provide evidence that co-designing queries and rubrics can make rubric-based RL a practical complement to RLVR beyond strictly verifiable tasks.
items.author: Rongzhi Zhang, Rui Feng, Zhihan Zhang, Jingfeng Yang, Qingyu Yin, Xin Liu, Zixuan Zhang, Priyanka Nigam, Bing Yin, Tuo Zhao, Chao Zhang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark

items.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
items.description: We describe AlignAtt4LLM, an IWSLT 2026 simultaneous speech translation system for English to German, Italian, and Chinese. The system is a synchronous cascade: Qwen3-ASR with forced alignment produces an incrementally updated source transcript, and Gemma-4 E4B-it translates that prefix under an MT-side AlignAtt policy. To our knowledge, this is the first application of AlignAtt to a decoder-only LLM, where the encoder-decoder cross-attention used by earlier AlignAtt systems is absent. We recove
items.summary: We describe AlignAtt4LLM, an IWSLT 2026 simultaneous speech translation system for English to German, Italian, and Chinese. The system is a synchronous cascade: Qwen3-ASR with forced alignment produces an incrementally updated source transcript, and Gemma-4 E4B-it translates that prefix under an MT-side AlignAtt policy. To our knowledge, this is the first application of AlignAtt to a decoder-only LLM, where the encoder-decoder cross-attention used by earlier AlignAtt systems is absent. We recover a usable policy by proposing (1) an explicit source span in the prompt, (2) offline selection of translation-specific alignment heads, (3) selective qk-fast replay of the draft-to-source attention block, and (4) runtime query/key capture that preserves model outputs bit-identically. On the IWSLT 2026 development set, AlignAtt4LLM outperforms the supplied baselines for the European target languages, English to German and English to Italian, in both the low-latency regime around 2 seconds and the high-latency regime below 4 seconds CU-LongYAAL. Results for English to Chinese are more mixed, but the method is not tied to Gemma-4: because AlignAtt4LLM only requires a deterministic prompt layout, calibrated attention heads, and query/key capture, the same policy can be reapplied to stronger translation-focused decoder-only MT backbones for non-European target languages.
items.author: Quentin Fuxa, Dominik Macháček
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, alignment

trendEntities.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
normalizedKeys: title:agentic chain of thought steering for efficient and controllable llm reasoning

items.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
items.description: Large language models improve final-answer accuracy through extended chain-of-thought reasoning, but often spend tokens inefficiently and offer little inference-time control. Existing efficient reasoning methods control thinking length by shortening, early-stopping, or compressing traces, leaving how the model thinks implicit. In this paper, we propose Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS), which formulates reasoning steering as a Markov decision process where a controller agent adaptively st
items.summary: Large language models improve final-answer accuracy through extended chain-of-thought reasoning, but often spend tokens inefficiently and offer little inference-time control. Existing efficient reasoning methods control thinking length by shortening, early-stopping, or compressing traces, leaving how the model thinks implicit. In this paper, we propose Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS), which formulates reasoning steering as a Markov decision process where a controller agent adaptively steers a frozen reasoner during inference. At each step, the controller observes the reasoning trace and remaining thinking budget, then issues a steering action consisting of a reasoning strategy and a steering phrase that initiates the next reasoner step. This enables budget-aware strategy control for efficient reasoning while preserving the reasoner's generation continuity. We initialize the controller agent from our constructed synthetic steering trajectories with multi-budget augmentation, and further optimize it via reinforcement learning with budget-conditioned reward shaping. Experiments across multiple benchmarks show that ACTS matches full-thinking performance with substantial token savings, and enables controllable accuracy-efficiency trade-offs across different reasoners and tasks. The code is available at https://github.com/Andree-9/ACTS.
items.author: Yu Xia, Zhouhang Xie, Xin Xu, Byungkyu Kang, Prarit Lamba, Xiang Gao, Julian McAuley

trendEntities.title: Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation
normalizedKeys: title:self refining agentic reinforcement learning for vision conditioned uav navigation

items.title: Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation
items.description: Deep reinforcement learning has shown strong potential for enabling autonomous robots to learn complex navigational tasks. However, its practical use still depends heavily on human designed reward functions and repeated manual fine tuning, which is time consuming and does not guarantee high success in the desired task. This paper presents AgenticRL, agent guided reinforcement learning framework that increases autonomy in reward design, policy refinement, and real world deployment for unmanned ae
items.summary: Deep reinforcement learning has shown strong potential for enabling autonomous robots to learn complex navigational tasks. However, its practical use still depends heavily on human designed reward functions and repeated manual fine tuning, which is time consuming and does not guarantee high success in the desired task. This paper presents AgenticRL, agent guided reinforcement learning framework that increases autonomy in reward design, policy refinement, and real world deployment for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) navigation tasks. AgenticRL uses a multimodal generative pre-trained tansformer (GPT) agent to interpret task information and visual scene observations, generate task specific reward functions, train policies using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm, and then act as a critic by evaluating the trained policy through diagnosis packets to generate feedback. Based on this feedback, the agent identifies failure modes and refines the reward function in a closed loop self improvement process. To further leverage the multimodal GPT agent during inference, AgenticRL uses real world images and natural language task information to automatically identify the active scenario and select the appropriate trained policy for execution. The framework is evaluated on multiple navigational tasks, including gate traversal, obstacle avoidance, wall barrier crossing with landing, trajectory following, and motion behavior learning. Experimental results show that the closed loop refinement process improves policy behavior compared with initial rewards by 71%. We also demonstrate sim-to-real transfer of the proposed framework, achieving a real world success rate of 91% and a sim-to-real accuracy of 94%.
items.author: Roohan Ahmed Khan, Yasheerah Yaqoot, Muhammad Ahsan Mustafa, Dzmitry Tsetserukou

This paper presents AgenticRL, agent guided reinforcement learning framework that increases autonomy in reward design, policy refinement, and real world deployment for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) navigation tasks. AgenticRL uses a multimodal generative pre-trained tansformer (GPT) agent to interpret task information and visual scene observations, generate task specific reward functions, train policies using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm, and then act as a critic by evaluating the trained policy through diagnosis packets to generate feedback. Based on this feedback, the agent identifies failure modes and refines the reward function in a closed loop self improvement process. To further leverage the multimodal GPT agent during inference, AgenticRL uses real world images and natural language task information to automatically identify the active scenario and select the appropriate trained policy for execution. The framework is evaluated on multiple navigational tasks, including gate traversal, obstacle avoidance, wall barrier crossing with landing, trajectory following, and motion behavior learning. Experimental results show that the closed loop refinement process improves policy behavior compared with initial rewards by 71%. We also demonstrate sim-to-real transfer of the proposed framework, achieving a real world success rate of 91% and a sim-to-real accuracy of 94%.

items.title: Using Reward Uncertainty to Induce Diverse Behaviour in Reinforcement Learning
items.description: Classical reinforcement learning (RL) typically seeks a deterministic policy that maximizes the expected sum of a scalar reward. Yet, modern applications such as language model fine-tuning or scientific discovery demand diversity. Existing remedies such as entropy regularization or diversity bonuses often require fragile trade-offs that sacrifice performance for stochasticity or rely on heuristic metrics that can misalign policy rankings. items.summary: Classical reinforcement learning (RL) typically seeks a deterministic policy that maximizes the expected sum of a scalar reward. Yet, modern applications such as language model fine-tuning or scientific discovery demand diversity. Existing remedies such as entropy regularization or diversity bonuses often require fragile trade-offs that sacrifice performance for stochasticity or rely on heuristic metrics that can misalign policy rankings. We argue that diversity is more naturally understood as the rational response to uncertainty in the reward. When the reward function is not perfectly known--as is the case with ambiguous preferences or imperfect reward models--committing to a single action can be sub-optimal. Building on this, we propose a fundamental reformulation of the RL objective by replacing the scalar reward with a distribution over reward functions, and applying a non-linear objective over sets of actions. The result is a framework in which calibrated behavioural diversity emerges naturally, remains controllable through the reward function distribution, and is obtained without sacrificing expected reward. Focusing on the contextual bandit setting, we derive a principled gradient estimator for this objective and prove that our formulation naturally generalizes both vanilla policy gradient and more recently developed action-set approaches. Our empirical results demonstrate that this framework offers a robust and theoretically grounded alternative for complex RL tasks where the traditional formulation of the problem fails to induce the desired breadth of agent behaviour.
items.author: Anthony GX-Chen, Ankit Anand, Gheorghe Comanici, Zaheer Abbas, Eser Aygün, David Smalling, Shibl Mourad, Doina Precup, André Barreto, Mark Rowland
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, fine-tuning
